title: Implement text/code renderer for TXT, Rust, and C++ documents
description:
Add a shared text/code renderer for plain-text and source-oriented issue-linked documents in the Web UI.

## Background

The agreed v1 behavior is split by mode: `.rs` and `.cpp` render with syntax highlighting and line numbers, while `.txt` renders as plain text with wrapping controls and line numbers. All three formats need inline rich/raw switching, search highlighting, and capped rich preview behavior for large files.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `.rs` and `.cpp` documents render with syntax highlighting and line numbers in the rich view.
- [ ] `.txt` documents render as plain text with wrapping controls and line numbers in the rich view.
- [ ] The renderer supports inline switching between rich and raw views for all three formats.
- [ ] Search queries highlight matching content in the rich view for TXT, Rust, and C++ documents.
- [ ] Large text/code files cap the rich preview and keep the full content available through the raw view.
- [ ] Renderer-specific tests cover syntax-highlighted source rendering, plain-text rendering, wrapping controls, search highlighting, and capped-preview behavior.

## Notes

Unknown text-like formats should still fall back gracefully rather than being misclassified as source files.
